name: Weihnachtsgans
recipeInstructions:
1. Remove the neck and giblets from the goose and set aside.
2. Remove as much visible fat from the cavity as possible.
3. Pat the goose dry, then rub with salt and pepper.
4. Place thyme, apples, celery, and onions in the cavity. Fasten the skin shut with poultry pins and secure tightly with twine. Fold the wings flat against the back and pin the neck skin to the back.
5. Place the goose breast-side down in a lightly oiled large, shallow roasting pan. Prick the skin all over with a fork.
6. Roast the goose uncovered for 30 minutes at 500°F, then reduce heat to 400°F and roast for an additional 30 minutes.
7. Carefully turn the goose breast-side up and roast uncovered for another hour.
8. Remove the pan from the oven and carefully prick the goose again with a fork. Add the remaining 2 cups of water to the pan and continue roasting uncovered for 1 to 1.5 hours, or until the internal temperature reaches 165°F.
9. Remove the goose from the pan and let it rest on a platter covered loosely with aluminum foil for 30 minutes before carving.
10. In a heavy skillet over low heat, heat 6 tablespoons of fat from the pan drippings. Add flour and cook, stirring constantly, until smooth.
11. Measure 3 cups of giblet juice (drippings) from the roasting pan and add to the skillet with broth. Cook, whisking constantly, until thickened.
12. Season with salt and pepper to taste. If desired, stir in finely chopped giblets.
Traditionally served with red cabbage and mashed potatoes.
